Risk Statement: Privileged vendor API access remains active after the analytics modernization project ended.
Control Failure: Least privilege and access recertification failed
Treatment: Revoke stale access, enforce quarterly vendor access certification, require named ownership
Evidence Required: Access review report, revoked token evidence, updated role matrix

Risk Statement: Contractor session credentials are exposed through infostealer malware and reused against vendor systems.
Root Cause: Weak endpoint hygiene and session governance
Treatment: Require phishing resistant multifactor authentication, endpoint attestation, contractor security clauses
Evidence Required: MFA report, endpoint compliance sample, contract addendum

Risk Statement: Expired vendor security assessments continue to support renewals without current evidence.
Root Cause: Review fatigue and poor expiration tracking
Treatment: Create supplier review calendar with blocking renewal gates and evidence standards
Evidence Required: Vendor review calendar, evidence checklist, renewal gate approval

Risk Statement: No centralized inventory provides a complete view of active vendors, data flows, and access paths.
Root Cause: Ownership ambiguity between Procurement, Legal, Security, and Compliance
Treatment: Build authoritative vendor inventory and require business owner attestation
Evidence Required: Inventory export, owner attestation, data flow map

Risk Statement: Temporary business exceptions become permanent and bypass required control enforcement.
Root Cause: Executive pressure and weak exception governance
Treatment: Implement expiring exception workflow with executive acceptance and compensating controls
Evidence Required: Exception log, expiration report, approval evidence

Risk Statement: Fragmented logs prevent rapid identification of unusual API activity and delay detection.
Root Cause: Monitoring design gaps and tool fragmentation
Treatment: Centralize API logs, tune alerts, create vendor anomaly use cases
Evidence Required: SIEM query results, log source inventory, alert tuning record

Risk Statement: Analysts ignore low fidelity alerts because daily noise overwhelms meaningful triage.
Treatment: Reduce false positives, define triage thresholds, track alert quality metrics
Evidence Required: Alert quality dashboard, triage playbook, false positive trend

Risk Statement: Incident disclosure is delayed because Legal, Communications, Compliance, and Security lack a clear escalation pathway.
Control Failure: Incident handling and communication controls failed
Treatment: Build breach communication matrix with decision authority and tabletop testing
Evidence Required: Tabletop report, contact tree, decision rights matrix

Risk Statement: Supplier contracts lack enforceable audit rights, breach notification timelines, and access evidence obligations.
Root Cause: Contract standards not aligned to current risk
Treatment: Refresh supplier security addendum and apply to Tier 1 renewals first
Evidence Required: Approved addendum, sample executed contract, gap tracker

Risk Statement: Security awareness training records show completion but do not prove changed behavior.
Root Cause: Training measured as attendance rather than control effectiveness
Control Failure: Awareness control measured the wrong outcome
Treatment: Measure simulated decision making, escalation speed, and exception discipline
Evidence Required: Simulation results, learning analytics, improved escalation metrics
